SEARCH
Module runtimeSE
Package runtimeSE

Interface Logger

public interface Logger
Interface Logger defines the API of a simple logging utility that emulates log4j logging.

Configuration of the logging utility is achieved by setting properties in the application.properties file:

  • logger.Runtime.threshold defines the minimum logging level to passed log messages to appenders. Possible values are:
    • FATAL - log only fatal messages.
    • ERROR - log only error and fatal messages.
    • WARN - log only warn, error and fatal messages.
    • INFO - log only info, warn, error and fatal messages.
    • DEBUG - log only debug, info, warn, error and fatal messages.
    • TRACE, ALL - log all messages.
  • logger.Runtime.appenders defines destinations of log messages. Possible values are console (for Console output) and names of log files.
Version:
"1.0.0-SNAPSHOT"
Author:
"sgraupner"

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    debug(String msg)
    Log a debug message.
    void
    error(String msg)
    Log an error message.
    void

    Log a fatal message.

    Return existing or new logger instance by name.

    void

  • Method Details

    • getLogger

      static Logger getLogger(String name)
      Return existing or new logger instance by name. Create logger instance when name is not yet preset.
      Parameters:
      name - name of the logger to return.
      Returns:
      logger instance of that name.

    • fatal

      void fatal(String msg)
      Log a fatal message. Fatal messages are the most severe messages that indicate a critical failure in the application and typically lead to application shut-down.
      Parameters:
      msg - message to log.

    • error

      void error(String msg)
      Log an error message. Error messages indicate a failure in the application that should be investigated and fixed, but typically do not lead to application shut-down. Exceptions that are typically logged as error messages.
      Parameters:
      msg - message to log.

    • warn

      void warn(String msg)
      Log a warn message. Warn messages indicate a potential problem in the application that does not cause a failure.
      Parameters:
      msg -

    • info

      void info(String msg)
      Log an info message. Info messages indicate a normal but significant event in the application.
      Parameters:
      msg - message to log.

    • debug

      void debug(String msg)
      Log a debug message. Debug messages indicate a detailed information about the application that is useful for debugging and troubleshooting.
      Parameters:
      msg - message to log.

    • trace

      void trace(String msg)
      Log a trace message. Trace messages indicate a very detailed information about the application that is useful for tracing the execution of the application.
      Parameters:
      msg - message to log.
